pattern: The law of cosines converts each side length directly into the angle sitting opposite it, so checking the triangle inequality first and then computing all three angles from that formula and sorting them handles every case.
hints: Three side lengths only form a triangle with positive area when each one is strictly less than the sum of the other two -- check this first and return an empty result immediately if it fails.

The law of cosines gives the angle opposite any side directly from all three side lengths: for the side of length a facing the unknown angle, with the other two sides b and c, the angle equals the arc-cosine of (b^2 + c^2 - a^2) divided by (2*b*c).
Compute the angle opposite each of the three sides this way, convert from radians to degrees, and sort the three results in non-decreasing order before returning them.

edge_inputs.description: a classic right triangle with clean angle values
side lengths that cannot form a triangle with positive area
a degenerate case where one side exactly equals the sum of the other two
an equilateral triangle where all three angles are equal
an isosceles triangle where two angles come out equal and must both appear in the sorted result
a very thin sliver triangle just barely satisfying the inequality
the largest side is listed first in the input, testing that the output order does not depend on input order

stress.description: random side lengths across the full constraint range, many of which will be invalid triangles
random valid triangles constructed to always satisfy the triangle inequality
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    b = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    c = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    lo = abs(b - c) + 1
    hi = min(1000, b + c - 1)
    if lo > hi:
        a = min(1000, b + c - 1) if b + c - 1 >= 1 else 1
    else:
        a = rng.randint(lo, hi)
    sides = [a, b, c]
    return [sides]

solutions.python:
import math
class Solution:
    def internalAngles(self, sides) -> list:
        a, b, c = sides
        if a + b <= c or b + c <= a or a + c <= b:
            return []
        angleA = math.degrees(math.acos((b * b + c * c - a * a) / (2 * b * c)))
        angleB = math.degrees(math.acos((a * a + c * c - b * b) / (2 * a * c)))
        angleC = 180.0 - angleA - angleB
        result = [angleA, angleB, angleC]
        result.sort()
        return result
